title: Lock v0.5 roadmap
reason: Lock the v0.5 roadmap after v0.4.0 post-release verification, ni-grill dogfood, roadmap/distribution alignment, and benchmark evidence expansion, while preserving Homebrew as a v0.5 candidate, model workspace packs as Experimental unless host-level install is verified, no-terminal as assisted, and the pre-runtime ni-kernel boundary.
proposed_changes: Update CAP-019 and REQ-019 so the v0.5 roadmap is framed as evidence, reliability, and adoption hardening rather than package-manager release work.
Add RISK-018 and strengthen existing risk/evaluation records for benchmark overclaiming, Homebrew availability, model workspace authority, no-terminal validation, and runtime drift.
Record DEC-017 with GRILL-001/002 addressed and GRILL-003/004/005 retained as v0.5 improvement or status notes.
Expand EVAL-023 evidence to include benchmark case files, ni status proof, demo checks, skill-pack checks, install/release checks, and prompt budget verification.
risk_impact: Adds an explicit high-severity v0.5 overclaim risk so benchmark evidence, Homebrew status, model workspace status, no-terminal claims, and downstream integration boundaries cannot drift into false availability or runtime claims.
readiness_impact: Expected to remain READY after CAP-019 has linked requirement, evaluation, mitigated high-severity risks, artifacts, accepted decision records, and no blocker questions.
